title: When Roald Dahl Wrote a Story Predicting the Rise of ChatGPT and Other AI Large Language Models (1954)
description: Most of us who know the work of Roald Dahl grew up with it, evenВӯtuВӯalВӯly comВӯing to conВӯsidВӯer the man a masВӯter of imagВӯiВӯnaВӯtive, often gr
body:
Most of us who know the work of Roald Dahl grew up with it, evenВӯtuВӯalВӯly comВӯing to conВӯsidВӯer the man a masВӯter of imagВӯiВӯnaВӯtive, often grotesque tales for chilВӯdren. A bit latВӯer on, when we heard that heвҖҷd also writВӯten books for adults, with titles like Kiss Kiss and Switch Bitch, some of us sought them out as a kind of forВӯbidВӯden litВӯerВӯary fruit. What tends to escape notice is that he also wrote for teenagers вҖ” or, in any case, that cerВӯtain of his stoВӯries were packВӯaged for teenagers into the posthuВӯmous volВӯume The Great AutoВӯmatВӯic GrammaВӯtiВӯzaВӯtor, whose title stoВӯry has gained a new relВӯeВӯvance in our age of ChatВӯGВӯPT, as explained in the new Tibees video above.
First pubВӯlished in 1954, вҖңThe Great AutoВӯmatВӯic GrammaВӯtiВӯzaВӯtorвҖқ conВӯcerns an enorВӯmousВӯly comВӯplex, wholВӯly anaВӯlog machine that can genВӯerВӯate page after page of text at a then-unimagВӯinВӯable clip. Its invenВӯtor, a beatВӯen-down young corВӯpoВӯrate employВӯee called Adolph Knipe, designs it based on the same prinВӯciВӯples heвҖҷd used to creВӯate an elecВӯtric calВӯcuВӯlaВӯtor that pleased his boss, Mr. Bohlen. A frusВӯtratВӯed writer of ficВӯtion by night, Knipe conВӯceives of the GrammaВӯtiВӯzaВӯtor as a tool of revenge against the magВӯaВӯzine indusВӯtry that spurned him. With the comВӯpaВӯnyвҖҷs backВӯing to build the thing, he tells Bohlen, they could domВӯiВӯnate the marВӯket for short stoВӯries almost withВӯout effort вҖ” and make their own presВӯtiВӯgious names as authors to boot.
вҖңIt stands to reaВӯson that an engine built along the lines of the elecВӯtric comВӯputВӯer could be adjustВӯed to arrange words (instead of numВӯbers) in their right order accordВӯing to the rules of gramВӯmar,вҖқ Dahl writes. вҖңGive it the verbs, the nouns, the adjecВӯtives, the proВӯnouns, store them in the memВӯoВӯry secВӯtion as a vocabВӯuВӯlary, and arrange for them to be extractВӯed as required. Then feed it with plots and leave it to write the senВӯtences.вҖқ Though Bohlen accepts the techВӯniВӯcal propoВӯsiВӯtion, he at first doubts the comВӯmerВӯcial one, at least until his employВӯee informs him that magВӯaВӯzines like the SatВӯurВӯday Evening Post, CollierвҖҷs, and LadiesвҖҷ Home JourВӯnal will pay for a stoВӯry вҖңanyВӯthing up to twenВӯty-five hunВӯdred dolВӯlarsвҖқ: nearВӯly $40,000 today.
Of course, 1954 was a difВӯferВӯent time. Today, theВ SatВӯurВӯday Evening Post, CollierвҖҷs, and LadiesвҖҷ Home JourВӯnalВ have all gone, as has the prospect of earnВӯing even a meaВӯger livВӯing through short stoВӯries. And a comВӯputВӯer of this kind, as Dahl describes it, would have been an enorВӯmous, noisy device laden with butВӯtons, dials, pedВӯals, and stops, each of which the вҖңwriterвҖқ would use to conВӯtrol such variВӯables as theme, style, tenВӯsion, humor, and pasВӯsion. вҖңThe qualВӯiВӯty may be infeВӯriВӯor,вҖқ an increasВӯingВӯly powВӯer-mad Knipe admits of the machineвҖҷs outВӯput, вҖңbut that doesnвҖҷt matВӯter. ItвҖҷs the cost of proВӯducВӯtion that counts.вҖқ All of us now posВӯsess GrammaВӯtiВӯzaВӯtors of our own, far faster, cheapВӯer, more verВӯsaВӯtile, and easВӯiВӯer to use than anyВӯthing Roald Dahl could have imagВӯined. Yet how many of us can hope to be read more than 70 years in the future?
via MetafilВӯter
RelatВӯed conВӯtent:
How George Orwell PreВӯdictВӯed the Rise of вҖңAI SlopвҖқ in NineВӯteen Eighty-Four (1949)
Sci-Fi Writer Arthur C. Clarke PreВӯdictВӯed the Rise of ArtiВӯfiВӯcial IntelВӯliВӯgence & the ExisВӯtenВӯtial QuesВӯtions We Would Need to Answer (1978)
Roald Dahl Gives a Tour of the Small BackВӯyard Hut Where He Wrote All of His Beloved ChildrenвҖҷs Books
Read a NevВӯer PubВӯlished, вҖңSubВӯverВӯsiveвҖқ ChapВӯter from Roald DahlвҖҷs CharВӯlie and the ChocoВӯlate FacВӯtoВӯry
Roald Dahl, Who Lost His DaughВӯter to Measles, Writes a HeartВӯbreakВӯing LetВӯter about VacВӯciВӯnaВӯtions: вҖңIt Is Almost a Crime to Allow Your Child to Go UnimВӯmuВӯnisedвҖқ
Based in Seoul,В ColВӯinВ MarshallВ writes and broadВӯcasts on cities, lanВӯguage, and culВӯture. HeвҖҷs the author of the newsletВӯterВ Books on CitiesВ as well as the booksВ н•ңкөӯ мҡ”м•Ҫ кёҲм§ҖВ (No SumВӯmaВӯrizВӯing Korea) andВ KoreВӯan Newtro.В FolВӯlow him on the social netВӯwork forВӯmerВӯly known as TwitВӯter atВ @colinmarshall.

title: How the Hagia Sophia Was Built, and How ItвҖҷs Being Saved from Collapse
description:
in Architecture, History |   	June 11th, 2026  Leave a Comment
	
			

	

Ask around for what everyВӯone knows about IstanВӯbul (othВӯer than that it us
body:
in Architecture, History |   	June 11th, 2026  Leave a Comment
	
			

	

Ask around for what everyВӯone knows about IstanВӯbul (othВӯer than that it used to be called ConВӯstanВӯtinoВӯple), and youвҖҷll find that the presВӯence of Hagia Sophia there comes right to many a mind. Less likeВӯly to be menВӯtioned is its proneВӯness to earthВӯquakes, though it tends to rank just below Tokyo on lists of cities under the greatВӯest threat from fault lines below. These two charВӯacВӯterВӯisВӯtics turn out to have a conВӯnecВӯtion, manВӯiВӯfest in the ongoВӯing seisВӯmic retroВӯfitting of IstanВӯbulвҖҷs symВӯbolВӯic catheВӯdral-turned-mosque-turned-museВӯum turned-mosque-again. Hagia Sophia is one of the most celВӯeВӯbratВӯed reliВӯgious buildВӯings standВӯing; keepВӯing it that way requires a seriВӯous engiВӯneerВӯing effort, as explained in the new B1M video above.
Since it was first built in the fourth cenВӯtuВӯry, Hagia Sophia has actuВӯalВӯly susВӯtained severe earthВӯquake damВӯage quite a few times, includВӯing a comВӯplete colВӯlapse of its cupoВӯla in the year 558 and parВӯtial colВӯlapsВӯes in the tenth and fourВӯteenth cenВӯturies. The conВӯstrucВӯtion of its famous cenВӯtral dome, along with the smallВӯer sub-domes that supВӯport it, gets a secВӯtion of its own in the video.
HostВ Fred Mills also gives due menВӯtion to the eight green marВӯble columns that supВӯport the upper floors of the catheВӯdral, thought to have been recyВӯcled from the ruins of the TemВӯple of Artemis (one of the SevВӯen WonВӯders of the Ancient World), and the red stone set into the floor on which emperВӯors were once crowned that would have been brought in from the EgyptВӯian desert.
In these and othВӯer respects, Hagia Sophia isnвҖҷt just a site of pilВӯgrimВӯage and worВӯship, but also a verВӯiВӯtaВӯble built record of cenВӯturies upon cenВӯturies of Roman, Greek, ChrisВӯtВӯian, and IslamВӯic civВӯiВӯlizaВӯtion. As eviВӯdenced by the scafВӯfoldВӯing curВӯrentВӯly up to facilВӯiВӯtate the project of readyВӯing it for the inevitable comВӯing of the big one вҖ” or rather, the bigВӯger one вҖ” the strucВӯture conВӯtinВӯues to change with time, though our era has an espeВӯcialВӯly strong conВӯcern for preВӯservВӯing what have by now become hisВӯtorВӯiВӯcal feaВӯtures. Hence the efforts now being put into restoraВӯtion: of the dome, natВӯuВӯralВӯly, but also of the floors, columns, and mosaics. If all goes well, Hagia Sophia will conВӯtinВӯue to stand as the most strikВӯing strucВӯture in IstanВӯbulвҖҷs already draВӯmatВӯic urban and geoВӯgraphВӯiВӯcal setВӯting for anothВӯer milВӯlenВӯniВӯum and a half, incorВӯpoВӯratВӯing hisВӯtoВӯry all the while.
RelatВӯed conВӯtent:
An IntroВӯducВӯtion to Hagia Sophia: After 85 Years as a MuseВӯum, ItвҖҷs Set to Become a Mosque Again
How the ByzanВӯtine Empire Rose, Fell, and CreВӯatВӯed the GloВӯriВӯous Hagia Sophia: A HisВӯtoВӯry in Ten AniВӯmatВӯed MinВӯutes
A CulВӯturВӯal Tour of IstanВӯbul, Where the Art and HisВӯtoВӯry of Three Great Empires Come TogethВӯer
360 Degree VirВӯtuВӯal Tours of the Hagia Sophia
Hear the Hagia SophiaвҖҷs Awe-InspirВӯing Acoustics Get RecreВӯatВӯed with ComВӯputВӯer SimВӯuВӯlaВӯtions, and Let YourВӯself Get TransВӯportВӯed Back to the MidВӯdle Ages
IstanВӯbul CapВӯtured in BeauВӯtiВӯful ColВӯor Images from 1890: The Hagia Sophia, TopВӯkaВӯki PalaceвҖҷs ImpeВӯrВӯiВӯal Gate & More
Based in Seoul,В ColВӯinВ MarshallВ writes and broadВӯcasts on cities, lanВӯguage, and culВӯture. HeвҖҷs the author of the newsletВӯterВ Books on CitiesВ as well as the booksВ н•ңкөӯ мҡ”м•Ҫ кёҲм§ҖВ (No SumВӯmaВӯrizВӯing Korea) andВ KoreВӯan Newtro.В FolВӯlow him on the social netВӯwork forВӯmerВӯly known as TwitВӯter atВ @colinmarshall.

title: David Bowie Picks His 12 Favorite David Bowie Songs
description: Admit it, your list of favorite Bowie songs is full of the big hits. Hell, maybe itвҖҷs all hits; thereвҖҷs no shame in that. DigВӯging deep into
body:
Admit it, your list of favorite Bowie songs is full of the big hits. Hell, maybe itвҖҷs all hits; thereвҖҷs no shame in that. DigВӯging deep into the crates will yield many an overВӯlooked surВӯprise, many a subВӯtle sleepВӯer, cut-up clasВӯsic, and elecВӯtronВӯic experВӯiВӯment. But if all youвҖҷve got is ChangesВӯbowieвҖ”the 1990 comВӯpiВӯlaВӯtion that became, for some genВӯerВӯaВӯtions, a definВӯiВӯtive stateВӯment of his careerвҖ”youвҖҷve still got a colВӯlecВӯtion of songs the likes of which have nevВӯer been heard before or since in modВӯern pop.
ComВӯpletists may grouch, but even resВӯiВӯdent Bowie scholars/local record store clerks have an вҖңAshВӯes to AshВӯes,вҖқ вҖңвҖҷHeroesвҖҷ,вҖқ вҖңChanges,вҖқ or вҖңModВӯern LoveвҖқ in their top ten. Whether ardent or casuВӯal fans, we conВӯnect with BowieвҖҷs music through mileВӯstones, both in his career and in our own lives. This truth has been exploitВӯed. In 2008, Mike Schiller at PopВӯMatВӯters bemoaned the fact that almost 20 Bowie comВӯpiВӯlaВӯtion albums had been released, a few of which вҖңdonвҖҷt realВӯly seem to court any greater purВӯpose whatВӯsoВӯevВӯer.вҖқ
GivВӯen this surВӯfeit of Bowie comВӯpiВӯlaВӯtions on the marВӯket, SchillerвҖҷs iniВӯtial groanВӯing reacВӯtion to news of yet anothВӯer (вҖңOh, good Lord. AnothВӯer David Bowie colВӯlecВӯtion?вҖқ) seems appoВӯsite. Except this colВӯlecВӯtion, iSEВӯLECT: BOWIE, released in 2008 to readВӯers of the U.K.вҖҷs Mail on SunВӯday, then latВӯer in an offiВӯcial CD and digВӯiВӯtal ediВӯtion, вҖңis actuВӯalВӯly someВӯthing speВӯcial.вҖқ Bowie вҖңpicked the trackВӯlist himВӯself. Even more than that, the trackВӯlist actuВӯalВӯly looksВ like someВӯthing heвҖҷd have picked himВӯself, rather than havВӯing a manВӯagВӯer or pubВӯliВӯcist pick it for him.вҖқ
iSEВӯLECT: BOWIE 
1. вҖңLife On Mars?вҖқ (from the album Hunky Dory)
2. вҖңSweet Thing/Candidate/Sweet ThingвҖқ (from the album DiaВӯmond Dogs)
3. вҖңThe Bewlay BrothВӯersвҖқ (from the album Hunky Dory)
4. вҖңLady GrinВӯning SoulвҖқ (from the album Aladdin Sane)
5. вҖңWinвҖқ (from the album Young AmerВӯiВӯcans)
6. вҖңSome AreвҖқ (curВӯrentВӯly excluВӯsive to this comВӯpiВӯlaВӯtion)
7. вҖңTeenage WildlifeвҖқ (from the album Scary MonВӯsters)
8. вҖңRepВӯeВӯtiВӯtionвҖқ (from the album Lodger)
9. вҖңFanВӯtasВӯtic VoyВӯageвҖқ (from the album Lodger)
10. вҖңLovВӯing The AlienвҖқ (from the album Tonight)
11. вҖңTime Will Crawl (MM Remix)вҖқ (new remix by David Bowie)
12. вҖңHang On To YourВӯself [live]вҖқ (from the album Live SanВӯta MonВӯiВӯca вҖҷ72)
See the full trackВӯlist above and hear a playlist of his picks at the top. If we put all our lists of favorites togethВӯer, we might see a very high perВӯcentВӯage of вҖңLife on Mars?вҖқ picks. WeвҖҷre in excelВӯlent comВӯpaВӯny; itвҖҷs BowieвҖҷs numВӯber one favorite song of his. But how many of his othВӯer picks might we choose? The eight-and-a-half minute вҖңSweet ThingвҖқ/вҖқCandidateвҖқ/вҖқSweet Thing (Reprise)вҖқ from DiaВӯmond Dogs? вҖңWinвҖқ from Young AmerВӯiВӯcansВ or вҖңThe Bewlay BrothВӯersвҖқ from Hunky Dory?
Aside from вҖңLife on Mars?вҖқ and the far lessВӯer-colВӯlectВӯed вҖңLovВӯing the AlienвҖқ and вҖңTime Will Crawl,вҖқ none of his twelve selecВӯtions were released as sinВӯgles. There are no songs from two of the most acclaimed Bowie albums, LowВ and вҖҷHeroesвҖҷ, unless we count вҖңSome AreвҖқ a bonus track includВӯed on the LowВ 1991 rereВӯlease. There are two tracks fromВ Lodger, the third and least accesВӯsiВӯble of his vauntВӯed Berlin trilВӯoВӯgy, and only one selecВӯtion from ZigВӯgy StarВӯdust,В and it ainвҖҷt вҖңZigВӯgy StarВӯdust.вҖқ
If anyВӯone else handВӯed you this list of favorite Bowie tracks, youвҖҷd be skepВӯtiВӯcal. Who puts вҖңHang On To YourВӯselfвҖқ (Live SanВӯta MonВӯiВӯca вҖҷ72) above any of the stuВӯdio tracks on that clasВӯsic 1972 breakВӯout album? David Bowie, thatвҖҷs who. And who knows, if youвҖҷd asked him the day before or after, he might have picked twelve difВӯferВӯent songs. ThereвҖҷs no telling how seriВӯousВӯly he took the exerВӯcise, but in the newsВӯpaВӯper release, he did вҖңcasuВӯalВӯly [pen] his inspiВӯraВӯtions for the songs and the recordВӯing processВӯes behind them,вҖқ notes AllmusicвҖҷs Jason LymanВӯgrover.
On his choice of вҖңTeenage Wildlife,вҖқ for examВӯple, Bowie comВӯmentВӯed: вҖңSo itвҖҷs late mornВӯing and IвҖҷm thinkВӯing, вҖҳNew song and a fresh approach. I know. IвҖҷm going to do a RonВӯnie SpecВӯtor. Oh yes I am. Ersatz just for one day.вҖҷ And I did and here it is. Bless. IвҖҷm still very enamВӯoured of this song and would give you two вҖҳModВӯern LoveвҖҷs for it anyВӯtimeвҖҰвҖқ Bowie got to expeВӯriВӯence his own music in a way no one else could. iSEВӯLECT: BOWIEВ gets behind the greatВӯest hits colВӯlecВӯtions for a glimpse at the way he heard and rememВӯbered his catВӯaВӯlogue.
Note: An earВӯliВӯer verВӯsion of this post appeared on our site in 2019.
RelatВӯed ConВӯtent:
David BowieвҖҷs 100 Must Read Books
The Art ColВӯlecВӯtion of David Bowie: An IntroВӯducВӯtion
How David Bowie Used William S. BurВӯroughsвҖҷ Cut-Up Method to Write His UnforВӯgetВӯtable Lyrics
David Bowie Sings ImpresВӯsions of Bruce SpringВӯsteen, Lou Reed, Iggy Pop, Tom Waits & More In StuВӯdio OutВӯtakes (1985)
Josh Jones is a writer and musiВӯcian based in Durham, NC.В

schema.headline: David Bowie Picks His 12 Favorite David Bowie Songs

title: 'Big Walk' Is a New Video Game about ... Walking and Talking
description: In the ever-expanding pantheon of open-world video games where combat, survival, crafting, and anarchy reign, the simple idea of taking a virtual
body:
In the ever-expanding pantheon of open-world video games where combat, survival, crafting, and anarchy reign, the simple idea of taking a virtual walk while chatting with a few friends might seem pointless. A new video game from Melbourne-based developer House House begs to differ, though, turning a casual stroll across dreamy landscapes into a uniquely collaborative game, where puzzles and the lengths required to solve them take center stage. Some areas of Big Walk render players speechless, forcing you to devise innovative ways to communicate. It might just be the antithesis of Fortnite or Grand Theft Auto.
This friendly, casual, and playful approach to game design may come as no surprise from the makers of the critically acclaimed Untitled Goose Game, which is centered entirely on a hapless goose that navigates everyday environments while avoiding the unwanted attention of nearby humans. вҖңAs much as Big Walk is a game about walking and talking, itвҖҷs also about exploring, and getting lost, and doing challenges, and sometimes, not really doing anything at all,вҖқ shares a game trailer.
You can play this вҖңcooperative online walker-talkerвҖқ on Steam, Switch 2, and PlayStation 5 beginning August 4.

schema.headline: 'Big Walk' Is a New Video Game about ... Walking and Talking

title: Domestic Arrangements Unfold in Kristof SantyвҖҷs Vivid Paintings
description: Known for his vibrant palettes and flattened perspectives, Belgian artist Kristof Santy translates common sights and everyday objects into vivid t
body:
Known for his vibrant palettes and flattened perspectives, Belgian artist Kristof Santy translates common sights and everyday objects into vivid tableaux. His paintings often highlight fruit and vegetables, tabletops, and modes of transportation, particularly those involved in industrial labor.
A new body of work continues SantyвҖҷs inquiries into the mundane, this time extending into fashions and furnishings. ThereвҖҷs a striped sweater vest with a nearly imperceptible wrinkle hanging from a rod and a modernist chair in fuchsia pushed against a kelly green wall. Earlier investigations appear, too, including a short, roll-up ladder dangling from the door of a helicopter as it hovers in the air sans operator.
вҖңFauteuilвҖқ (2025), oil on canvas, 150 x 120 centimeters
This new body of work will be on view this fall at Sorry WeвҖҷre Closed in Brussels. Until then, find more of SantyвҖҷs work on Instagram.
вҖңIjscoupeвҖқ (2026), oil on canvas, 180 x 150 centimeters
вҖңCompositie met groentenвҖқ (2025), oil on canvas, 200 x 160 centimeters
вҖңHelikopterвҖқ (2025), oil on canvas, 220 x 200 centimeters
A glimpse of SantyвҖҷs studio
SantyвҖҷs studio

title: Delcy Morelos Tends to Sepulchral Installations in a Divine Connection to the Land
description: The Colombian artist Delcy Morelos describes her hometown of Tierralta as вҖңa paradise full of butterflies and unpaved streets.вҖқ In the late 1960s
body:
The Colombian artist Delcy Morelos describes her hometown of Tierralta as вҖңa paradise full of butterflies and unpaved streets.вҖқ In the late 1960s and early вҖҷ70s, Morelos spent her days in her grandmotherвҖҷs garden, running barefoot and gleaning what it meant to live in connection with the land. When paramilitary and guerrilla troops moved in, though, the region was plunged into a chaotic state of grief and fear.
In her earliest works, Morelos translated the death and destruction plaguing her home into two-dimensional compositions. As she details in a new segment for Art21, acrylic painting was not long her primary mode of working, and quickly, she returned to the earth, incorporating soil, straw, and grass into large-scale installations. The film follows the artist as she installs a sepulchral mound in SevilleвҖҷs Centro Andaluz de Arte ContemporГЎneo, a former Carthusian monastery turned art museum that once housed Christopher ColumbusвҖҷ remains.



Throughout the film, we witness Morelos grab gobs of straw-laden soil and affix the chunky material to a standing structure, which eventually grows a range of produce native to both sides of the Atlantic. In contrast to the spaceвҖҷs colonial ties, the artistвҖҷs work presents a way of creating and living thatвҖҷs entwined in natural rhythms. Visitors are greeted by notes of cinnamon, cloves, and fecund soil before being enveloped by towering walls of growth. Within the vaulted monastery, MorelosвҖҷ indomitable forms offer a direct tie to the sacred and divine right beneath our feet.
вҖңMany people believe they are in a bubble, and that is why they can do things that harm nature, harm others, and also harm themselves,вҖқ she says, demonstrating a profound sense of care for and connection to all that gives life.
This segment is part of Art21вҖҷs Human Nature episode and is available to watch on its site. Find additional films on YouTube.

schema.headline: Delcy Morelos Tends to Sepulchral Installations in a Divine Connection to the Land

title: Natural Dyes Merge with Mixed Media in Annalise Neil's Dreamy Cyanotypes
description: вҖңMatter is memory, and memory is a medium,вҖқ says artist Annalise Neil, whose surreal cyanotypes brim with animals, fungi, geological specimens, sh
body:
вҖңMatter is memory, and memory is a medium,вҖқ says artist Annalise Neil, whose surreal cyanotypes brim with animals, fungi, geological specimens, shells, and more that she augments with watercolor. Recently, the artist has been adding rich, earthy tones with natural dyes such as wild strawberry leaf, oak gall, loquat leaf, and chestnut. She has used botanical teas to shift the natural blue color of the cyanotypes for quite a while, but the sepia tonality has emerged as a larger focus lately, which allows her to layer hues like browns and purples.
NeilвҖҷs experiences in nature profoundly influence her individual pieces in a process that she poetically describes as вҖңmelting, rolling, pinching, sanding, walking across meadows, cheek on sun-warmed boulders.вҖқВ This year, sheвҖҷs a resident artist at VolcanВ Mountain Foundation in Julian, California, which merges artistic and scientific inquiry. вҖңI endeavor to create work that will lead to contemplation and reflection and that invites a thoughtful examination of our relationship to reality and our surroundings,вҖқ she says. 
вҖңLittoral TalismanвҖқ (2026), watercolor and cyanotype toned with madder root, chestnut tannins and strawberry leaf tea on Canson Montval paper, hand-carved wood panel, gouache, volcano keyhole limpet shell, copper, 28 x 18 x 1.75 inches
вҖңFor my site-specific work, I begin by hiking for many days and photographing intriguing things I find, including birds andВ mammals, plants, geological forms, and insects,вҖқ she says. вҖңAs I photograph specimens in wild and cultivated spaces, I capture a brief version of their existence that I transmute into a negative and then into a cyanotype.вҖқ The images are then supported on hand-carved wooden panels.
NeilвҖҷs work is currently on view in Fast Forward: Analog Photography as a Third Space at the Los Angeles Center for Photography and Sanguine Glimmers at Hey Books! in San Diego, among others. See more and follow updates on the artistвҖҷs Instagram.
вҖңIntervalsвҖқ (2023), watercolor and cyanotype on Awagami Mitsumata paper mounted to acrylic-painted wood panel, 10 x 8 x 1.5 inches
вҖңAuguriesвҖқ (2025), cyanotype and chestnut tannins on Hahnemuhle Sumi-e paper mounted to hand-carved wood panels, kiln-cast glass sculptures, flashe, bronze, 18 x 32 x 2 inches
From the вҖҳIdyllwild ReverieвҖҷ series (2025), cyanotype toned with strawberry leaf and chestnut, watercolor, and acrylic on wood panel, 12 x 24 x 1 inches
вҖңLoopholeвҖқ (2024), bleached and toned cyanotype, watercolor, flashe, kiln-cast glass, handmade wood panel, bronze, 14.5 x 16 x 2 inches
вҖңChaparral CoronetвҖқ (2026), cyanotype on Hahnemule Sumi-e paper toned with chestnut and strawberry leaf, watercolor, and shellac ink on hand-carved wood panel, 12 x 12 x 1.75 inches
вҖңLocusвҖқ (2024), kiln-cast glass, cuttlefish-cast bronze, toned cyanotype, watercolor, handmade wood panel, flashe, bronze wire, 14 x 10.5 x 1.5 inches
вҖңHarmony IIвҖқ (2024), watercolor and toned cyanotype on cotton sateen mounted to wood panel, 30 x 24 x 1.5 inches
вҖңGravityвҖқ (2024), bleached and traditional cyanotype, watercolor, kiln-cast glass, cuttlefish-cast bronze, handmade wood panel, flashe, 16 x 8 x 3.5 inches
вҖңLight EmergentвҖқ(2026), cyanotype on Hahnemule Sumi-e paper toned with strawberry leaf, watercolor, and pinyon pine pitch on hand-built and carved wood panel, 25 x 18 x 1 inches

schema.headline: Natural Dyes Merge with Mixed Media in Annalise Neil's Dreamy Cyanotypes
